title: Query Multiple Columns
summary: Learn how to query multiple columns using SELECT in SQL.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to Querying Multiple Columns
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, you can retrieve data from multiple columns using the SELECT statement. This allows you to view specific data from a table. For example, if you want to see the name and price of products, you can use:

```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products;
```

This query will return the 'name' and 'price' columns from the 'products' table. Try it yourself by modifying the columns you want to retrieve.

sketchBlocks.title: Worked Example: Retrieving Product Information
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Let's say you want to retrieve the 'name', 'category', and 'stock' of products. You can write the query as follows:

```sql
SELECT name, category, stock FROM products;
```

This query will display the product name, category, and stock quantity for each product in the 'products' table. Line by line, the query specifies:
- `SELECT name, category, stock`: The columns you want to retrieve.
- `FROM products`: The table to query.

Your turn: Try adding the 'price' column to this query and run it in the SQL editor.

quizDraft.title: Basic SELECT Query
quizDraft.prompt: Which SQL statement retrieves the 'name' and 'price' columns from the 'products' table?
quizDraft.hint: Focus on the correct syntax for selecting specific columns.

help.concept: The SELECT statement is used to specify which columns to retrieve from a table.
help.hint_1: Ensure the columns are listed after SELECT and before FROM.
help.hint_2: The correct syntax is `SELECT column1, column2 FROM table_name;`.

quizDraft.title: Understanding SQL Syntax
quizDraft.prompt: What does the following SQL query do? `SELECT category, stock FROM products;`
options: It retrieves all columns from the 'products' table.
It retrieves the 'category' and 'stock' columns from the 'products' table.
quizDraft.hint: Look at which columns are specified in the SELECT statement.

help.concept: The SELECT statement specifies which columns to retrieve.
help.hint_1: Check the columns listed after SELECT.
help.hint_2: The query retrieves only the columns specified after SELECT.

quizDraft.title: Selecting Multiple Columns
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following queries correctly select multiple columns from the 'products' table? (Choose all that apply)
options: SELECT name, category, price FROM products;
quizDraft.hint: Consider queries that specify multiple columns in the SELECT statement.

help.concept: Multiple columns can be selected by listing them after SELECT.
help.hint_1: Look for queries that list more than one column after SELECT.
help.hint_2: Queries that use `SELECT *` retrieve all columns, not specific ones.

quizDraft.title: Identifying Correct SQL Queries
quizDraft.prompt: Which queries will retrieve the 'name' and 'category' columns from the 'products' table? (Choose all that apply)
options: SELECT name, category, price FROM products;
quizDraft.hint: Focus on queries that include both 'name' and 'category' in the SELECT statement.

help.concept: The SELECT statement specifies which columns to retrieve.
help.hint_1: Ensure both 'name' and 'category' are listed after SELECT.
help.hint_2: Queries that list only one of the columns are not correct.

quizDraft.title: Order SQL Query Components
quizDraft.prompt: Arrange the components of a SQL query to select the 'name' and 'price' columns from the 'products' table.
quizDraft.hint: Start with the SELECT keyword followed by the columns.

help.concept: The order of SQL query components is crucial for correct syntax.
help.hint_1: The query starts with SELECT, followed by the columns.
help.hint_2: The FROM keyword follows the column list.

quizDraft.title: SQL Query Structure
quizDraft.prompt: Reorder the SQL query components to retrieve 'category' and 'stock' from the 'products' table.
quizDraft.hint: Begin with SELECT and list the columns next.

help.concept: SQL queries have a specific structure that must be followed.
help.hint_1: The query starts with SELECT, followed by the columns.
help.hint_2: The FROM keyword comes after the column list.

quizDraft.title: Fill in the SQL Query
quizDraft.prompt: Complete the SQL query to select 'name' and 'stock' from the 'products' table: `SELECT [blank1] FROM products;`
quizDraft.hint: List both columns separated by a comma.

help.concept: Multiple columns are separated by commas in the SELECT statement.
help.hint_2: Use a comma to separate the column names.

quizDraft.title: SQL Query Completion
quizDraft.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the query: `SELECT [blank1] FROM products;` to retrieve all columns.
quizDraft.hint: Use the wildcard character to select all columns.

help.concept: The asterisk (*) is used to select all columns in a table.
help.hint_1: The wildcard character (*) selects all columns.

quizDraft.title: Query Product Names and Prices
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to retrieve the 'name' and 'price' columns from the 'products' table.

quizDraft.title: Retrieve Product Categories
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the 'category' and 'stock' columns from the 'products' table.

quizDraft.title: Select All Product Information
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to retrieve all columns from the 'products' table.
